acquisition method: The mass spectrometer was operated in positive and data-dependent mode
alkylation reagent: alkylation was done with 55 mM Iodoacetamide
alkylation concentration: alkylation was done with 55 mM Iodoacetamide
cleavage agent: trypsin digestion of the SOM and IOM
collision energy: ions selected for MS/MS scan were fragmented using higher energy collision dissociation (HCD) at normalized collision energy of 28%
fractionation method: After 1D SDS-PAGE electrophoresis and trypsin digestion
fragmentation method: ions selected for MS/MS scan were fragmented using higher energy collision dissociation (HCD)
instrument: using an on-line system consisting of a nano-pump UltiMate™ 3000 UHPLC binary HPLC system coupled to a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer
ionization type: The mass spectrometer was operated in positive and data-dependent mode
labeling: we quantified the relative abundance of proteins using two label-free methods
reduction reagent: Reduction was performed using 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT)
reduction concentration: Reduction was performed using 10 mM dithiothreitol (DTT)
